name: Blind Lemon Jefferson
abstract: Lemon Henry "Blind Lemon" Jefferson (September 24, 1893 – December 19, 1929) was an American blues and gospel singer-songwriter and musician. He was one of the most popular and successful blues singers of the 1920s and has been called the "Father of the Texas blues".

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: All the old records of the old blues singers like Bessie Smith, Blind Lemon Jefferson.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Jazz was about all that we heard in Kentucky at that time, the blues, Bessie Smith, Mamie Smith, Blind Lemon Jefferson, Leadbelly…
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: He wasn’t an old time blues singer with a guitar walking the streets, you know, sort of like Blind Lemon Jefferson.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Eurreal Wilford "Little Brother" Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: Did you ever hear him from Texas? ... Yeah, but I never did care nothing about his singing or whatever.

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Cage and Robert Pete Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to Blind and that one's Jefferson. You know him? I got his records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Cage and Robert Pete Williams Interview, 1967-05-13

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: And do you know that I had something to do with him making records? Years later?
